title: 12 Approval of Tasks/Transactions
body_text:
12	Approval of Tasks/Transactions
12.1	Overview of Approval of Tasks/Transactions
In
PaymentStreamâ¢
AFT, approval is required if the user who initiated a task is set up to require approval and/or if the Originatorâs monthly limits are set to one million dollars or more for their organization. Approval permissions are user-based and set within User Management. See the User Management Part for procedures on setting up
PaymentStream
AFT users and defining AFT permissions.
Mandatory forced approval is when a financial institution or business member has one or more Originator IDs and the monthly accumulative limit is one million dollars or more. All Originator IDs for that financial institution or business member will require approval. Mandatory forced approvals are automatically enforced within
PaymentStream
AFT. For more information on limits and forced approvals, see Chapter 14, Managing Risk on
PaymentStream
AFT.
Tasks that require approval show as Pending Tasks in
PaymentStream
Direct. Users with approval permission receive an email advising of tasks that require their attention. Pending tasks expire after seven calendar days.
Approval of tasks is required as follows:
For Data Entry Automatic Release â When a record is added or edited.
For Data Entry Manual Release â When transactions are released.
For File Upload â When a file is uploaded.
Scheduled Notifications for Pending Task Emails
Data Entry Automatic Release originators who have a high volume of added or edited records have the option to receive scheduled email notifications for Pending Tasks.
Note:	Calculate your equivalent time based on the times provided in Pacific Time.
This feature provides three Pending Task email notifications to approvers daily (instead of one email per action) at 10:30 am PT; 1:00 pm PT; and 3:30 pm PT. This schedule provides enough time to alert approvers of tasks that require action before the AFT automatic release extraction times that occur as follows:
2:00 am PT
4:00 am PT
7:00 am PT
11:00 am PT
1:30 pm PT
3:00 pm PT
4:00 pm PT
Automatic Release originators who chose to receive scheduled Pending Task email notifications can still log into
PaymentStream
Direct to check and view their Pending Tasks or provide approvals for submitted tasks. If an AFT record is added or edited and requires an immediate approval for same day release, the AFT originator is responsible for setting up an internal procedure to ensure their approvers are aware of any essential immediate action.
This process doesnât impact the AFT confirmation emails sent when transactions are validated on
PaymentStream
AFT. PS AFT users will continue to receive confirmation emails when transactions are released.
To request scheduled Pending Tasks notifications for your financial institution or on behalf of a Data Entry Automatic Release business member, contact Central 1 Support via ServiceNow. See Section 1.4, Contacts Websites, and Other Resources.
Related Information
For procedures on how to approve tasks, see the following guides:
PaymentStream
AFT â Guide for Automatic Release,
Form 5598
PaymentStream
AFT â Guide for Manual Release,
Form 5599
PaymentStream
AFT â Guide for File Upload,
Form 5600
END
